weeks.label: Jul 5–11
weeks.bikeNote: Bike every day is fine — 9km/day (4.5km each way) from the new apartment is a light load, well under what used to be extra zone 1 territory at the old place.
weeks.swimNote: Monday: active swim class. Use it as your weekly warm-up — improves shoulder mobility and breathing.
weeks.tip: Week 1 with Monday swim: your first run shifts to Tuesday. You have 4 total sessions — that's the maximum load for this phase.

training.title: Swim class
training.detail: Swim class as scheduled — counts as aerobic cross-training. Don't add a run today.

days.movement: Bike to the pool is fine — TSB is -6.5 today, mild residual load, trending toward neutral over the next two rest days. First session, no need to conserve.
days.food: Normal balanced breakfast 90 min before. Post-swim: protein + fruit (yogurt, shake). Base phase: moderate carbs, no special loading.
days.bigPicture: First session of the 15-week plan. The swim is deliberate: aerobic cross-training that builds base and shoulder mobility without loading the running legs. TSB sits at -6.5 with two rest days left before Monday — mild residual load, nothing concerning, and it should ease further by the time training starts. HRV baseline is still building (first reading: 65ms), so future updates will track day-to-day recovery more precisely.

training.title: Adaptation run
training.detail: 30 min at easy pace (5:10–5:20/km) — calibrate how you feel, first session of the plan

days.movement: U-Bahn both ways today — no need to load the legs further right after the adaptation run.
days.food: Normal breakfast. Pre-run: light snack 90 min before if needed. Post-run: protein + carbs within 30 min (eggs and toast, or a recovery shake). Build the fueling habit now.
days.bigPicture: First run of the plan — an adaptation run to calibrate effort after Monday's swim. 30 min at 5:10–5:20/km, lean toward the slower end: TSB is -25.8 today, still carrying fatigue from Saturday's 22.75km effort and a short 2h sleep Sunday. HRV (71ms, 8% above the 66ms baseline) and sleep (score 95) are both strong, so this is residual load, not poor recovery — it should clear over the next few easy days.

training.title: Easy run + strides
training.detail: 35 min easy + 4×80m progressive accelerations at the end (not sprints, just smooth pick-ups)

days.movement: U-Bahn both ways today — strides need fresh legs even though they're short. TSB was -31.7 Tuesday, deeper fatigue than earlier estimated, so don't expect it fully cleared by Thursday even after Wednesday's rest day.
days.food: Normal carb-moderate day, no special loading. Pre-run: light snack 90 min before if training after work. Post-run: normal meal with protein priority.
days.bigPicture: Third session of Week 1 base, coming after Wednesday's full rest day gave the legs a break from Monday's swim-plus-commute load. TSB was -31.7 Tuesday morning — past the -25 threshold, deeper fatigue than first estimated (a data bug had understated this week's real training load). One rest day won't fully clear it; expect this run to feel heavier than a normal easy session. Strides (4×80m progressive accelerations) add neuromuscular work without aerobic stress — smooth and controlled, not fast. This session primes the legs for Saturday's first long run.

training.title: Long run
training.detail: 45 min continuous, easy pace (5:10–5:20/km) — ~8 km

days.movement: Weekend — no work commute either way. U-Bahn or walk to the start, no cycling before — preserve fresh legs, especially after last week's long run overran its prescription.
days.food: Pre-run carbs 1-2h before — oats or toast with banana. Bring a gel or dates to test your gut for the first time — 55 min is long enough to start practicing. Post-run: protein + carbs within 30 min.
days.bigPicture: 55 minutes, ~9-10km — a step up from last week's nominal 8km, but last week's actual long run already ran to 70 minutes/12.45km at tempo-effort HR, so this is really about consolidation, not a new stretch. Two weeks of easy running plus a first MP session should have volume trending the right way if the extra fatigue from last weekend's apartment move has cleared in time.

weeks.label: Jul 12–18
weeks.bikeNote: Thursday (intervals): ride to the office as usual, but EASY. Don't warm up your legs before the intervals — save energy for the session.
weeks.swimNote: Monday swim: perfect as active recovery after Saturday's long run. Do laps at moderate pace, no sprinting.
weeks.tip: Marathon pace (4:15–4:20/km) should feel challenging but manageable. If you're gasping, slow down — fitness comes from consistency.

training.title: Swim class
training.detail: Swim class — aerobic pace, technique focus. Don't push: it's active recovery from last week.

days.movement: Bike to the pool is fine — active recovery, legs should have loosened up nicely after the weekend long run.
days.food: Normal balanced day — light on heavy fats, focus on hydration after the weekend.
days.bigPicture: Active recovery day opening week 2, still base phase. The pool loosens legs after Saturday's first long run while keeping the aerobic engine warm. Week 1 carried real fatigue (TSB as deep as -31.7, and still -27.1 on Jul 9) from Jul 4's big untracked effort — by now, with Friday's rest day and an easy week 1 close behind you, it should be substantially cleared, though a fresh dip right after Saturday's long run is normal. Week 1 was as much about clearing that debt as adapting to the plan.

training.title: Easy run
training.detail: 35 min at aerobic pace (5:00–5:15/km)

days.movement: U-Bahn both ways today — TSB is still deep negative (-24.3) after a weekend spent moving apartments (Fri/Sun loading a truck and carrying furniture up and down stairs) plus Saturday's long run that ran ~55% past its prescribed distance. Protect the legs even on an easy day.
days.food: Normal balanced day, base-phase carb levels — nothing special needed for a 35-minute easy effort.
days.bigPicture: Week 2 of base, easy Tuesday session as the aerobic base keeps widening. TSB is still -24.3 heading in, carrying over fatigue from a weekend apartment move (Friday and Sunday both spent loading a truck and carrying furniture up and down stairs) plus Saturday's long run overrun — this week's easy days need to actually stay easy given three of the last four tracked running sessions ran at tempo-effort HR despite on-target pace. Sets up Thursday's first marathon-pace contact (3×5' at 4:15–4:20/km).

training.title: First contact with MP
training.detail: 40 min: 10' wu + 3×5' at marathon pace (4:15–4:20/km) + 90'' walking rec + 10' cd

days.movement: U-Bahn both ways — TSB is at -22.9 as of Tuesday, still deep negative from last week's move-weekend load and Saturday's long-run overrun. First real MP intervals aren't the day to add 9km of cycling on top.
days.food: Carb breakfast 1.5h before — first real MP contact deserves fueled legs. Evening: rice or pasta with protein, recovery starts at the table.
days.bigPicture: Week 2 of base, about 87 days out from race day. 3×5' is deliberately short — the goal is neuromuscular familiarity with 4:16/km, not fitness stress. TSB sits at -22.9 today, so treat how the legs feel as the real signal, not the calendar; it should keep climbing back toward neutral through the rest of the week.

training.title: Long run
training.detail: 55 min continuous, easy pace (~9–10 km)

days.movement: No cycling before the long run — get to the start on foot or U-Bahn. No commute to plan around, it's Saturday.
days.food: Carb-loaded breakfast 1–2h before. Bring a gel or dates to test your gut for the first time — 55 min is long enough to start practicing before race-day fueling matters.
days.bigPicture: 55 minutes, ~9–10km, about 85 days out from race day. A step up from last week's 8km. Closing out week 2 of base with the trend clearly toward more volume: two long runs, a first MP session, and consistent easy running behind you already, with TSB still working its way back from this week's -22.9 low.

weeks.label: Jul 19–25
weeks.bikeNote: Thursday morning: easy bike to the office. Evening after intervals: bike home is fine, helps active leg recovery.
weeks.swimNote: Swim class ended last Monday (Jul 13) — no more Monday class. Starting this week, open swim moves to Wednesday: self-managed, 30–35 min aerobic pace between Tuesday's run and Thursday's intervals.
weeks.tip: 1km repeats build muscular memory at marathon pace. Take the full recovery: it's not weakness, it's correctness.

days.movement: Bike both ways fine — TSB should be well clear of this week's -22.9 low by now, and an easy 35' shouldn't ask much of the legs either way.
days.food: Normal balanced day, base-phase carb levels.
days.bigPicture: Week 3 of base, about 82 days out from race day. These mid-week easy runs are the backbone of the plan — keep them genuinely easy, especially after last week's pattern of easy days running at tempo-effort HR despite on-target pace. Three weeks into base without a miss, the aerobic engine is quietly getting bigger.

training.title: Open swim
training.detail: 30–35 min in the pool, aerobic pace. Continuous crawl with focus on breathing. No interval training.

days.movement: Bike both ways to the pool is fine — low-impact enough not to matter for recovery, first self-managed session now that the class has ended.
days.food: Normal balanced day.
days.bigPicture: First self-managed open swim, about 81 days out, two weeks earlier than planned since the class wrapped up last Monday. Placed between Tuesday's easy run and Thursday's 1km repeats — active recovery without running impact, same role Monday's swim used to play.

training.title: 1km repeats
training.detail: 45 min: 10' wu + 4×1km at 4:20/km + 90'' rec + 10' cd — first real quality session

days.movement: Bike both ways — short 9km round trip, manageable even after intervals now that TSB has had a week to recover from this week's -22.9 low.
days.food: Carb-rich breakfast before. Post-session: protein + carbs within 30 min of finishing.
days.bigPicture: First 1km repeats at marathon pace, about 80 days out from race day. Each rep builds the neuromuscular memory your legs need for km 25–42. Three weeks of base behind you now, with TSB back near neutral after this week's dip — take the full recovery between reps and let the pace feel controlled.

training.title: Long run
training.detail: 60 min (~11 km), easy pace

days.movement: No bike before the long run. U-Bahn or rest completely the morning of — no commute to plan around, it's Saturday.
days.food: Carb breakfast before. Keep testing your race gel or dates — train the gut alongside the legs.
days.bigPicture: 60 minutes, about 11km now, roughly 78 days out from race day. Your aerobic base is building week by week. Third week of base without a miss, and this week's -22.9 TSB low from the move-weekend/long-run overrun should be fully cleared by now — this builds fitness on a clean base rather than digging out of fatigue.

weeks.label: Jul 26 – Aug 1
weeks.bikeNote: Recovery week: daily bike is fine, actually helpful. But no efforts on the bike — no hills, no fast pace. Take the U-Bahn if it rains.
weeks.swimNote: Open swim continues on Wednesday — recovery week, so keep it light and easy, same as the running sessions.
weeks.tip: Recovery week: never skip it. The body adapts during rest. Higdon calls it the 'invisible' week — the most important one.

training.title: Recovery run
training.detail: 25 min very slow (5:20–5:30/km) — recovery week

days.movement: Bike both ways fine — very easy run, no leg stress
days.food: Normal balanced day
days.bigPicture: Recovery week, about 75 days out from race day. Slower than feels necessary, which is exactly right. The body is consolidating three weeks of base building, including working through this cycle's -22.9 TSB dip, before the next load increase.

training.title: Open swim
training.detail: 25–30 min easy pool, recovery week — don't push, just move.

days.movement: Easy bike to pool — recovery week, no restrictions.
days.food: Normal day — recovery week means eating normally, not less.
days.bigPicture: Second self-managed open swim, kept light for recovery week. Same Wednesday slot that will carry into build phase from next week.

training.title: Easy run + strides
training.detail: 30 min + 4×80m accelerations

days.movement: Bike both ways fine
days.food: Normal balanced day
days.bigPicture: Light strides keep your legs sharp during recovery week. No fatigue, just neuromuscular activation.

training.title: Reduced long run
training.detail: 45 min — let the body absorb the last 3 weeks of load

days.movement: Easy bike to start your run warm-up, or U-Bahn — recovery week, no pressure
days.food: Normal pre-run breakfast. Recovery week: no need for aggressive fueling.
days.bigPicture: Recovery long run — shorter than week 3. This is where your body actually gets fitter: not during hard weeks, but in the recovery after them.

weeks.label: Aug 2–8
weeks.bikeNote: Monday stays a rest day, as it has since week 3. Bike all other days is fine. Friday evening (before long run): easy bike, or U-Bahn if your legs are tired.
weeks.swimNote: Wednesday open swim continues — between Tuesday's run and Saturday's long run. 30–35 min of moderate crawl, no sprints. Note: if today you bike to work (9km) + bike to pool (11km) = 20km total — drink and eat well, and keep it in mind for Thursday.
weeks.tip: You've been managing Wednesday at the pool yourself for two weeks now. Goal stays the same: active recovery and aerobic capacity, not performance in the water.

training.title: Easy run
training.detail: 38 min (5:00–5:10/km) — Monday is now full rest

days.movement: Bike both ways fine — first build week, legs should feel fresh after Monday rest
days.food: Normal balanced day — build phase starts, slightly more carbs overall
days.bigPicture: Welcome to Build phase. Volume increases from here. Today is easy — but the sessions later this week and beyond will demand more.

training.title: Open swim
training.detail: 30–35 min in the pool, aerobic pace. Continuous crawl with focus on breathing. No interval training.

days.movement: Bike to work + bike to pool = ~20km today. Manage it consciously — eat well and hydrate.
days.food: High carb day to fuel the 20km total biking + swim. Rice, pasta, or potatoes at lunch.
days.bigPicture: Wednesday remains your cross-training anchor, same as the last two weeks. Pool between Tuesday's run and the weekend long run is the perfect placement — active recovery without running impact.

training.title: Long run with MP
training.detail: 70 min: first 50 at easy pace, last 15 at 4:20/km (~13–14 km total)

days.movement: No cycling before the long run. U-Bahn to your starting point.
days.food: Pre-run carb breakfast. Bring a gel — you'll be out 70 minutes. Test your race nutrition.
days.bigPicture: First long run with a marathon-pace finish. The last 15 minutes at 4:20/km while tired is exactly what km 30–42 will feel like. This is where sub-3h is built.

weeks.label: Aug 9–15
weeks.bikeNote: Thursday: U-Bahn both ways — even the shorter 9km round trip is extra load on post-session legs. Friday: bike fine, but don't push.
weeks.swimNote: Wednesday in the pool: between Tuesday's run and Thursday's intervals. The ideal time for water — recover from Tuesday and prepare legs for Thursday. Doing both work and the pool today is 20km round trip if by bike — if you want fresher legs for tomorrow's intervals, do the whole loop by U-Bahn instead.
weeks.tip: 15 km long run: bring gels or dates. Your gut needs training just as much as your legs — testing race nutrition in training is part of the plan.

training.title: Easy run
training.detail: 40 min

days.movement: Bike both ways fine
days.food: Normal balanced day
days.bigPicture: Easy run mid-week. The build phase is ramping — these easy days protect your ability to hit Thursday hard.

training.title: Open swim
training.detail: 35 min, aerobic crawl. You can add 4×50m slightly faster at the end if you feel good.

days.movement: 20km round trip (work + pool) by bike, or the whole loop by U-Bahn if you'd rather save legs for Thursday's intervals — don't mix modes mid-loop.
days.food: Carb-focused day. Rice or pasta at lunch — you need fuel for 20km biking plus tomorrow's hard session.
days.bigPicture: Swimming the day before intervals is deliberate — water loosens your legs without adding running fatigue. Don't push hard in the pool today.

training.title: 1.5km repeats
training.detail: 55 min: 10' wu + 4×1.5km at 4:20/km + 2' walking rec + cd

days.movement: U-Bahn both ways today — non-negotiable, legs need to be fresh after the intervals.
days.food: Good carb breakfast 1.5h before. Post-session: protein + carbs within 30 min.
days.bigPicture: 4×1.5km at marathon pace — your longest quality reps so far. Sustained pace demands full recovery between reps. If you're on target by rep 3, you're fit.

training.title: Long run
training.detail: 80 min (~15 km), easy pace (5:00–5:15/km)

days.movement: No bike before. U-Bahn to your run start or rest fully before heading out.
days.food: Pre-run carbs. Gel at 45 min. Post-run: protein + carbs immediately.
days.bigPicture: 80 minutes — 15km of easy running. Bring gels and treat this as race nutrition practice. Your gut adapts too, not just your legs.

weeks.label: Aug 16–22
weeks.bikeNote: 17 km long run = legs engaged. Monday return to normal cycling.
weeks.swimNote: Wednesday: legs recovering from Tuesday, preparing for Thursday. Swimming is the perfect tool — no impact, heart working. With 20km on the bike (work + pool), this is your aerobically heaviest day of the week — manage it consciously.
weeks.tip: The progression teaches your body to accelerate when already tired — exactly what happens at km 30 of a marathon.

training.title: Easy run
training.detail: 40 min

days.movement: Bike both ways fine
days.food: Normal balanced day
days.bigPicture: Easy run. Mid-build phase — you're handling more volume now than most runners ever attempt. Keep these easy days genuinely easy.

training.title: Open swim
training.detail: 35 min aerobic. Try 200m crawl / 1' rest × 4–5 sets — builds endurance with no leg impact.

days.movement: 20km round trip by bike today, or the whole loop by U-Bahn if you want to manage the load before Thursday's hard session — pick one, don't split it.
days.food: Carb-heavy day — you're biking 20km, swimming, and running hard tomorrow. Pasta or risotto for dinner.
days.bigPicture: Aerobically your heaviest day of the week: biking, swimming, and tomorrow comes the tough session. Manage Wednesday consciously — it sets up Thursday.

training.title: MP progression
training.detail: 55 min: 15' wu + 20' at 4:30/km then 10' at 4:15/km (real MP) + cd

days.movement: U-Bahn both ways today — protect the legs after the progression run.
days.food: Good carb breakfast. Dinner: protein + carbs to start recovery for Saturday's 17km.
days.bigPicture: Progression run: start at 4:30/km, accelerate to 4:15/km while already tired. This is exactly what km 30–35 will demand. If you hit the splits, you're on track.

training.title: Long run
training.detail: 90 min (~17 km), easy pace

days.movement: No cycling before. Full rest from bikes on Saturday morning.
days.food: Pre-run carbs. Gel at 45–50 min. Post-run: protein + carbs immediately.
days.bigPicture: 90 minutes — 17km. You're in serious long run territory now. Each km you finish at easy pace is a deposit in your sub-3h bank account.

weeks.label: Aug 23–29
weeks.bikeNote: Recovery week: daily bike is fine and beneficial. Helps move the legs without stressing them. No restrictions.
weeks.swimNote: Optional swim this week. If you're tired, skip it — rest is worth more than a pool session. Recovery week: if you do go, take the U-Bahn round-trip for work that day (save your legs).
weeks.tip: Second recovery week. Don't add km to compensate. The last 3 weeks have packed adaptation into your legs — let it consolidate.

training.title: Recovery run
training.detail: 30 min very slow — mid-block recovery

days.movement: Bike both ways fine — recovery week, no restrictions
days.food: Normal balanced day — eat enough, don't undereat in recovery
days.bigPicture: Recovery week. Very slow run — deliberately slower than feels right. Your body is absorbing 3 weeks of serious build work.

training.title: Swim (optional)
training.detail: Recovery week: 25 min of light swimming is fine, or skip if the body feels tired. Nothing forced.

days.movement: If you go to the pool, take U-Bahn round-trip for work today — save your legs this week
days.food: Normal balanced day
days.bigPicture: Optional pool session. If you feel tired, skip without guilt. Recovery weeks are for recovery — the training benefit comes from rest, not volume.

training.title: Easy run + strides
training.detail: 35 min + 5×80m progressive

days.movement: Bike both ways fine — easy session
days.food: Normal balanced day
days.bigPicture: Light strides keep sharpness without adding fatigue. In recovery week these are the most important neuromuscular stimulus you do.

training.title: Reduced long run
training.detail: 60 min — active recovery

days.movement: Easy bike before is fine this week — it's a recovery long run
days.food: Normal pre-run breakfast. No gel needed for 60 min.
days.bigPicture: 60-minute recovery long run. Shorter than your recent runs by design. The adaptations from weeks 5–7 lock in this weekend.

weeks.label: Aug 30 – Sep 5
weeks.bikeNote: Thursday: hardest session of the block. U-Bahn both ways — don't add 4.5km on already-stressed muscles. Friday: U-Bahn — save legs for the 100 min long run.
weeks.swimNote: Wednesday in the pool before Thursday's hard intervals. Don't fatigue yourself — it's preparation, not training. With 20km on the bike Wednesday (work + pool), take the U-Bahn both ways Thursday — fresh legs for the 3×2km.
weeks.tip: The long run with 30 min at MP is the key session for sub-3h. You learn to run fast when already tired — exactly km 30–42.

training.title: Easy run
training.detail: 42 min

days.movement: Bike both ways fine
days.food: Normal balanced day
days.bigPicture: Back from recovery week — legs should feel surprisingly good. Last build block before Peak phase. Make every session count.

training.title: Open swim
training.detail: 35–40 min. This week add 6×50m fast at the end — simulates the effort you'll do in Thursday's intervals.

days.movement: 20km total bike + swim. Take U-Bahn both ways Thursday to compensate.
days.food: Carb-heavy day. Pasta or risotto for dinner — you have hard intervals tomorrow.
days.bigPicture: Wednesday prep for Thursday's hardest session yet. Don't go all-out in the pool — it's priming, not training. 6×50m fast at the end wakes up your anaerobic system.

training.title: 2km repeats
training.detail: 60 min: 10' wu + 3×2km at 4:15–4:20/km + 2'30'' rec + cd — race section simulation

days.movement: U-Bahn both ways today — non-negotiable.
days.food: Best carb breakfast of the week 1.5h before. Post-session: protein + carbs immediately.
days.bigPicture: 3×2km at marathon pace — the longest quality reps of Build phase. Each rep is a 2km race at your sub-3h target. If you hit the splits, the fitness is there.

training.title: Long run with MP finish
training.detail: 100 min: first 60 easy, last 30 at 4:20/km — first 'quality long run'

days.movement: No cycling before. U-Bahn to your run start. This is your most important session yet.
days.food: Carb-load Friday evening. Pre-run breakfast. Gel at 45 min and 70 min.
days.bigPicture: The signature build session: 100 minutes with the last 30 at marathon pace. This is exactly what km 30–42 will demand. Nail this and you know you can go sub-3h.

weeks.label: Sep 6–12
weeks.bikeNote: Peak phase: daily bike fine but ONLY easy pace. Thursday: U-Bahn both ways — legs are needed for the intervals.
weeks.swimNote: Wednesday: swimming as active recovery between Tuesday and Thursday. 35 min max, nothing fast. Goal: loosen legs, don't add fatigue. Peak Phase: 20km on the bike (work + pool) still adds up before Thursday's intervals — do the whole work+pool loop by U-Bahn instead if you want fresher legs.
weeks.tip: Peak phase: if you feel unusual pain, don't ignore it. One rest day now is worth 2 weeks of downtime later. Sub-3h is built by staying healthy.

training.title: Easy run
training.detail: 45 min

days.movement: Easy bike both ways — peak phase, but easy runs don't need U-Bahn
days.food: Normal balanced day with slightly higher carbs — peak phase demands more glycogen
days.bigPicture: Welcome to Peak phase. Volume is at its highest — protect your health above all else. Any unusual pain gets a rest day, not a push through.

training.title: Swim — active recovery
training.detail: 35 min aerobic crawl. Peak phase: swimming is now primarily recovery, not additional training.

days.movement: 20km round trip (work + pool) by bike, or the whole loop by U-Bahn to manage fatigue before Thursday — pick one, don't mix modes mid-loop.
days.food: Carb-focused day. Peak phase means your energy needs are at their highest.
days.bigPicture: Swimming in Peak phase is recovery, not training. 35 minutes of easy crawl loosens legs without adding stress before Thursday's long tempo.

days.movement: U-Bahn both ways today.
days.food: Best carb breakfast. Post-session recovery meal within 30 min.
days.bigPicture: 40 continuous minutes at near-marathon pace. This builds your lactate threshold — the key physiological marker for sub-3h. It's supposed to feel uncomfortable.

training.title: Long run
training.detail: 110 min (~20–21 km), easy pace

days.movement: No cycling. Full morning off the bike. U-Bahn to your run start.
days.food: Carb-load Friday night. Pre-run breakfast. Gel at 45 min and 80 min. Post-run: full recovery meal.
days.bigPicture: 110 minutes — 20–21km. You're running the longest distances of the plan. Every km at easy pace is building the aerobic base that carries you through 42km race day.

weeks.label: Sep 13–19
weeks.bikeNote: Hardest week of the plan. Thursday: U-Bahn BOTH ways — the 3×3km needs completely fresh legs, no bike today. Monday: easy bike to loosen up.
weeks.swimNote: Wednesday between the hardest sessions. LIGHT swim — 30 min max, easy crawl. No extra efforts in the water this week. Hardest week of the plan: U-Bahn round-trip for work Wednesday, and separately bike round-trip to the pool (or do both round-trips the same way if you prefer) — don't do 20km today.
weeks.tip: 22–23 km is your training maximum. You don't need more: the marathon is completed through 15 weeks of work, not one single session.

training.title: Easy run
training.detail: 45 min

days.movement: Easy bike both ways — save the U-Bahn for Thursday
days.food: Normal balanced day with high carbs — hardest week ahead
days.bigPicture: The hardest week of the entire plan starts now. Keep Tuesday easy — you need everything you have for Thursday's 3×3km.

training.title: Swim — active recovery
training.detail: 30–35 min light swim. Legs heavy from Tuesday? Water is your best friend today.

days.movement: U-Bahn round-trip for work, and separately bike round-trip to the pool — max 11km cycling today. No 20km.
days.food: High carb day. Pasta for dinner — tomorrow is the hardest session of the plan.
days.bigPicture: Light swim before the hardest session of the year. 30 minutes of easy crawl — no more. Tomorrow you need 100% of your legs for 3×3km.

training.title: 3km repeats
training.detail: 65 min: 10' wu + 3×3km at 4:15/km + 3' rec + cd — hardest session of the plan

days.movement: U-Bahn BOTH ways. No bike at all today. No exceptions.
days.food: Best carb breakfast of the training block. Post-session: full recovery meal immediately.
days.bigPicture: The hardest session of the entire plan. 3×3km at sub-3h race pace. If you hit all three reps on target, you have the fitness for October 11.

training.title: Peak long run
training.detail: 120 min (~22–23 km) with last 20 min at 4:20/km — your longest run ever

days.movement: No cycling. U-Bahn both ways. This is your longest training run ever.
days.food: Carb-load Friday evening. Pre-run breakfast. Gel every 40–45 min. Full recovery meal after.
days.bigPicture: 120 minutes — 22–23km, your training maximum. After this, it's about protecting your fitness, not building it. You've already done the work.

weeks.label: Sep 20–26
weeks.bikeNote: Post-peak recovery week: daily bike is fine and very beneficial. Helps keep circulation going and loosen legs after the 22 km.
weeks.swimNote: Wednesday: swimming as therapy. You can spend just 20 min in the water. The goal is to come out feeling lighter than when you went in. Post-peak recovery week: 20km on the bike today is ok — running load is reduced.
weeks.tip: Third recovery week. The body is consolidating the 22 km and 3km repeats. You may feel 'out of shape' — it's a feeling, not reality.

training.title: Recovery run
training.detail: 35 min very slow — mandatory unload after peak week

days.movement: Easy bike both ways — recovery week, legs benefit from gentle movement
days.food: Eat well — don't reduce calories after your hardest week. Your body is rebuilding.
days.bigPicture: Mandatory recovery after the hardest week of the plan. Very slow run — slower than you think you need. Your legs are consolidating 3×3km and 22km.

training.title: Swim — unload
training.detail: 30 min relaxed swimming. Backstroke, floating, whatever feels good. Pure recovery.

days.movement: 20km biking today is fine — running load is reduced this week
days.food: Normal balanced day
days.bigPicture: 20–30 minutes of water therapy. Backstroke, easy crawl, floating. Come out feeling lighter. Pure recovery, no training goal.

training.title: Easy run
training.detail: 35 min, free pace without watching the watch

days.movement: Bike both ways fine
days.food: Normal balanced day
days.bigPicture: Run without looking at your watch. This recovery week is for letting go — the hard work is done, now you consolidate.

training.title: Reduced long run
training.detail: 70 min — recovery from peak

days.movement: Easy bike before is fine this recovery week
days.food: Normal pre-run breakfast. No gel needed for 70 min in recovery week.
days.bigPicture: 70-minute recovery long run — a significant step down from 120 minutes. Your body is storing the adaptations from peak week. You may feel 'flat' — that's normal and temporary.

weeks.label: Sep 27 – Oct 3
weeks.bikeNote: Thursday (3×10' at 4:16/km): U-Bahn BOTH ways — this is the definitive proof you can run sub-3h, legs fresh at 100%. Friday: bike fine (4.5km one way). Saturday before long run: U-Bahn.
weeks.swimNote: Wednesday: last 'serious' swim session. From here on, swim only if you feel like it — taper starts in a few days. Thursday you have 3×10' at 4:16/km: don't do 20km on the bike Wednesday — U-Bahn round-trip for work, and separately bike round-trip to pool (11km).
weeks.tip: The 3×10' at exactly 4:16/km give you definitive proof: you have the fitness for sub-3h. From here, protect the work you've done.

training.title: Easy run
training.detail: 40 min

days.movement: Easy bike both ways
days.food: Normal balanced day, start thinking about pre-race nutrition strategy
days.bigPicture: Final push before taper. Two hard sessions this week, then the volume drops for good. Stay healthy — every training day counts double now.

training.title: Swim — last serious session
training.detail: 35 min. Last time swimming counts as training. After this, recovery only.

days.movement: U-Bahn round-trip for work, and separately bike round-trip to pool (11km). No 20km today before Thursday's race simulation.
days.food: Carb-load today and tomorrow — Thursday is your final proof session.
days.bigPicture: Last serious swim session. After today, pool is optional recovery only. Enjoy the routine — taper starts in days.

training.title: Final race simulation
training.detail: 55 min: 10' wu + 3×10' at 4:16/km (EXACT sub-3h pace) + cd — last hard session

days.movement: U-Bahn BOTH ways. No cycling at all. Fresh legs only.
days.food: Best carb breakfast of the week. This is your race rehearsal day.
days.bigPicture: 3×10' at exactly 4:16/km — the definitive fitness test. If you hit all three intervals, you have the fitness for sub-3h on October 11. After today, you protect what you've built.

training.title: Moderate long run
training.detail: 90 min (~17 km) at easy pace — last real long run

days.movement: U-Bahn to your run start. No cycling before.
days.food: Pre-run carbs. Gel at 50 min. Post-run: recovery meal.
days.bigPicture: Last real long run. 90 minutes at easy pace — just maintaining aerobic feel. From here the volume drops. You've done the work. Protect it.

weeks.label: Oct 4–10
weeks.bikeNote: Tapering: easy bike fine Monday–Wednesday. Thursday (quality session): U-Bahn. Friday–Saturday: U-Bahn or walk — legs to preserve for Sunday.
weeks.swimNote: Wednesday swim optional — only if you feel good and not tired. 20 min of easy arm work is enough. Don't add water kilometers.
weeks.tip: Tapering: legs feel heavy or strange? That's normal — the body is 'loading up'. Eat well, sleep a lot, avoid standing for long periods.

training.title: Easy run
training.detail: 30 min, free pace — enjoy the lightness

days.movement: Easy bike both ways — taper week, legs should feel surprisingly light
days.food: Start increasing carb percentage this week. Pasta, rice, bread.
days.bigPicture: Taper week. Legs may feel unnaturally good or strangely flat — both are normal. Your body is loading up glycogen. Run easy and enjoy the reduced volume.

training.title: Swim (optional)
training.detail: 20–25 min light swim if you feel good. If you're tired, skip without guilt.

days.movement: Easy bike both ways — or skip cycling entirely, it's taper week
days.food: Carb-heavy day. Pasta for dinner.
days.bigPicture: Optional pool session. 20 minutes of easy arm movement is enough. If you're tired, skip it — the race is in 3 days.

training.title: Last quality session
training.detail: 35 min: 10' wu + 3×1km at 4:16/km + 90'' rec + cd — short but at race pace

days.movement: U-Bahn both ways today — save legs for Sunday.
days.food: Good carb breakfast. Start carbo-loading tonight — pasta, rice, potato. 8–10g carbs/kg today.
days.bigPicture: Final quality session before the race. 3×1km at 4:16/km — short, sharp, and confidence-building. These legs are ready. After today, rest.

training.title: Pre-race jog
training.detail: 15 min very easy — move the legs, don't tire them

days.movement: No cycling today or tomorrow. U-Bahn everywhere. Protect your legs completely.
days.food: Day –1: light but carb-rich dinner, nothing new. Porridge or toast with honey and banana tomorrow morning.
days.bigPicture: Pre-race jog. 15 minutes to shake out the legs and calm the nerves. Nothing more. Lay out your kit tonight. Sleep early. Tomorrow is race day.

weeks.label: Oct 11 — RACE
weeks.bikeNote: Saturday before race: U-Bahn or taxi. No bike. Sunday post-race: whatever gets you home — just don't run.
weeks.swimNote: No pool from Thursday onwards. Final recovery is rest, carbo-loading, and sleep only.
weeks.tip: Sub-3h strategy: do NOT start fast. Runners who finish sub-3h run the second half faster than the first. Control the ego at km 1–10.

training.title: MUNICH MARATHON — SUB 3H
training.detail: Start at 4:20/km for the first 5 km (even if it feels slow). Hold 4:16 to km 35. Then give everything. No bike, no pool today — just run.

days.movement: No bike. Get to the start by foot or public transport. After the race: whatever gets you home.
days.food: Race morning: porridge or toast with honey, banana + coffee 2–3h before. Gel every 5–6km from km 15. Celebrate after.
days.bigPicture: Race day. 15 weeks of work comes down to this. Start at 4:20/km even if it feels slow — it is slow, on purpose. Runners who finish sub-3h run the second half faster than the first. Trust the training.
